description_long: This object consists of a rectangular bale of straw or hay, tightly bound with two metallic straps running lengthwise across its surface. Resting atop the bale is a pitchfork with a long, straight wooden handle and a metallic head featuring four evenly spaced tines. Next to the bale lies a brown, cylindrical sack or bag, partially collapsed and appearing to be made of fabric or leather. The straw bale has a rough, jagged texture at its ends, with straw pieces protruding irregularly. The metallic straps are slightly reflective and contrast with the matte, fibrous appearance of the straw. The overall scene suggests a utilitarian setup, commonly found in urban gardens, parks, or maintenance areas.
description: A straw bale bound with metal straps, topped by a four-tined pitchfork with a wooden handle, and accompanied by a brown sack.
description_view_0: The front view shows the pitchfork embedded in the top of the straw bale, with the brown sack resting on the ground in front.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the full length of the straw bale, the two metal straps, and the brown sack lying to the left.
description_view_2: From this angle, the pitchfork's tines and handle are visible atop the bale, with the brown sack partially obscured behind the bale.
description_view_3: The rear view shows the pitchfork and bale from behind, with the brown sack mostly hidden and only its end visible.
